## Consensus zone
- Email marketing remains relevant and delivers measurable ROI for B2B companies
- Personalisation improves engagement metrics across most industry segments

## Contested zone
1. AI personalisation is overhyped relative to implementation costs and complexity
2. Privacy regulations fundamentally change email viability and list management

## Outlier positions
- Email is obsolete for Gen Z buyers who prefer social and messaging channels
- Email will be entirely AI-generated within 5 years, removing human creativity

## Evidence weight summary
The weight of evidence supports continued email relevance with growing personalisation importance. ROI data is strong but implementation complexity is underreported.

## The unresolved question
Can AI personalisation overcome privacy friction and deliver sustainable competitive advantage for mid-market companies?
